activities_projects.title: Constitutional Check & Balance Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the relationship between the Governor and the Council of Ministers
steps: Research why the Governor is an 'appointed' head while the CM is an 'elected' head
Analyze a scenario where a Governor might reserve a bill for the President's consideration
Discuss the potential for conflict if the State Government and Central Government belong to different parties
Write a report on the Governor's role as a 'bridge' between the Center and the State
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Evaluates the federal structure and the importance of constitutional non-elected roles in oversight
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of analysis regarding the neutral role of the Governor (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Coalition Government & Stability Simulation
activities_projects.objective: Understand the complexities of multi-party governance
materials: Hypothetical election data with 5 parties (none reaching 50%)
steps: Assign 'ideologies' to each party (e.g., Party A focus on farmers, Party B on industry)
Negotiate a 'Common Minimum Program' where at least two parties agree to work together
Draft a list of challenges a CM faces in keeping a coalition together compared to a majority party
Explain the term 'Horse-trading' and why it is seen as a threat to democracy
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Analyzes the practical political challenges of forming and sustaining a government without a single-party mandate
activities_projects.evaluation: Feasibility of the Common Minimum Program and logic of the stability analysis (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Legislative vs. Executive Responsibility Audit
activities_projects.objective: Differentiate between political and administrative accountability
steps: Trace the path of a policy from an MLA's question in the House to its implementation by a District Collector
Identify who is responsible if the policy fails: The Minister or the civil servants?
Define 'Collective Responsibility' in the context of the Cabinet
Create a diagram showing the hierarchy from the CM down to the village-level officer
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Distinguishes between the permanent executive (officials) and the political executive (ministers)
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of the accountability chain and conceptual clarity (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The 'Zero Hour' Debate Simulation
activities_projects.objective: Practice critical questioning and spontaneous defense in the assembly
materials: Current news reports on state-level issues
steps: Assign students as Opposition MLAs to find three critical flaws in a recent government decision
Assign a student as the relevant Minister to prepare a defense using 'Departmental Data'
Simulate a 10-minute session where the Speaker must control a heated argument
Write a summary of how the 'power of questioning' forces the government to be alert
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Demonstrates how the Legislative Assembly acts as a check on the powers of the Government
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of the questions asked and the factual accuracy of the defense (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Media Bias & Transparency Inquiry
activities_projects.objective: Evaluate the role of the Fourth Estate in state governance
materials: Two different newspaper reports on the same assembly session
steps: Compare how a 'pro-government' and an 'opposition-leaning' newspaper headline the same event
Identify 'omissions' (what was left out) in both reports
Draft a short 'Ethics Code' for journalists attending a government press conference
Explain how social media has changed the way people put pressure on their MLAs
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Critically assesses the influence of media in shaping public perception of government work
activities_projects.evaluation: Critical thinking in identifying bias and the logic of the Ethics Code (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Budgetary Allocation Project
activities_projects.objective: Understand how the government prioritizes spending
materials: Summary of your state's current year budget
steps: Calculate the percentage of the budget spent on 'Social Services' (Health/Education) vs 'General Services' (Police/Administration)
Identify if the budget addresses the problems raised in the Patalpuram case study
Propose three changes to the budget to favor the poor or marginalized sections
Discuss why governments often find it difficult to satisfy every department's needs
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Relates the financial decisions of the government to the welfare of the common citizen
activities_projects.evaluation: Statistical accuracy in percentage calculation and quality of proposals (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Bill to Law: The Legislative Gauntlet
activities_projects.objective: Trace the rigorous process of making a state law
materials: A draft of a hypothetical 'Environment Protection Bill'
steps: Simulate the 'Three Readings' of the bill in the assembly
Include a stage where the 'Select Committee' reviews the bill and suggests changes
Identify the role of the Governor in giving 'Assent' to the bill
Explain what happens if a Governor returns a bill for reconsideration
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the multi-layered scrutiny required to pass a law in a democracy
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct sequencing of the legislative process (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Speaker's Neutrality Challenge
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the importance of the Speaker's role in a divided house
steps: Research the process of electing a Speaker and why they usually come from the ruling party
Discuss the 'Anti-Defection Law' and the Speaker's role in it
Write a 100-word essay on: 'Can a Speaker truly remain neutral in a political debate?'
Identify the powers of the Speaker to suspend an unruly member
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Evaluates the balance between political affiliation and constitutional duty
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical depth of the essay and accuracy of the Speaker's powers (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Constituency Redrawing (Delimitation) Study
activities_projects.objective: Understand the geographical basis of representation
steps: Explain why constituencies are redrawn every few years (Delimitation)
Using a map, argue if one large constituency should be split into two for better governance
Identify the challenges an MLA faces in a very large or sparsely populated area
Discuss how 'Voter Turnout' reflects the success of an MLA's work
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Analyzes the link between geography, population density, and effective representation
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical reasoning for the proposed boundary changes (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The 'Shadow Cabinet' Exercise
activities_projects.objective: Understand the role of the Opposition in detailed governance
steps: Assign 'Shadow Ministers' from the students to monitor specific departments (Health, Education, Agriculture)
Each Shadow Minister must find one failure and one success of the actual government in that field
Draft a 'Shadow Report' suggesting better alternatives for the existing policies
Explain why a strong Opposition is essential for a healthy democracy
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Synthesizes the proactive role of the Opposition in providing constructive criticism
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of the policy alternatives proposed in the Shadow Report (10 marks)
